hero.heading: Non-Residential Premises Loans for Your Business Growth
hero.tagline: Pingal Capital — Finance Offices, Shops & Commercial Spaces with Ease.

form.title: Apply for Non-Residential Loan
form.subtitle: A dedicated advisor will contact you within one business hour.

info.heading: What Is a Non-Residential Premises Loan?
info.paragraph: A Non-Residential Premises Loan (also called a Commercial Property Loan) is a dedicated financing solution for purchasing, constructing, expanding, or renovating business and commercial properties such as offices, shops, showrooms, clinics, warehouses, or other non-residential premises. It gives entrepreneurs and business owners the capital they need to grow their operations without burdening working capital.

eligibility.heading: Who Is Eligible?
eligibility.subtext: Non-Residential Premises Loans are typically available for:

items.question: What types of non-residential properties are eligible for a loan?
items.answer: We finance offices, retail shops, warehouses, industrial units, and mixed-use commercial properties. The property must be legally registered and free from disputes.

items.question: What is the maximum loan amount I can avail?
items.answer: Loan amounts typically range from ₹10 lakhs to ₹10 crores, depending on the property value, your repayment capacity, and lender terms.

items.question: What is the usual tenure for a non-residential premises loan?
items.answer: Tenures generally range from 5 to 15 years, giving you flexibility to manage EMIs as per your business cash flow.

items.question: Can I get a loan for a property I plan to rent out commercially?
items.answer: Yes, rental-income-generating commercial properties are eligible. Lenders often factor in projected rental income while assessing your repayment capacity.

items.question: How long does the approval process take?
items.answer: Our advisors typically complete the initial assessment within one business day. Full loan approval, subject to documentation and verification, usually takes 7–15 working days.
